summary: This is a brief job posting rather than a traditional blog post. Joel Spolsky announces that Fog Creek Software is actively seeking an experienced Win32 C/C++ developer to work on their Copilot product. The post leverages Joel's blog audience as a recruiting channel, appealing directly to skilled systems programmers.
key_points: Fog Creek is continuously hiring good programmers, suggesting a permanent talent search mentality
The specific need is for Win32 (C/C++) expertise on the Copilot team, a remote desktop assistance product
Joel uses his popular blog as a direct recruiting tool, bypassing traditional job boards
money_quotes: We're always looking for good programmers at Fog Creek, but right now we could really, really use a top notch Win32 (C/C++) developer to join the Copilot team.
key_insight: A well-known tech blog doubles as a powerful recruiting channel, letting companies reach exactly the kind of skilled developers they need.
